data.title: Eli Lilly hikes dividend by 15.4% to $1.50 a share; approves $15B share repurchase program
data.description:
• Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) declares $1.50/share quarterly dividend, 15.4% increase from prior dividend of $1.30.
  • Forward yield 0.75%
  • Payable March 10; to shareholders of record Feb. 14; ex-div Feb. 14.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 15.4% after paying a quarterly dividend of $1.30 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • Additionally, the board of Eli Lilly (LLY) has approved a new $15 billion share repurchase program. The company's prior $5 billion share repurchase program was completed in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • See LLY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Tuesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
6:00 AM NFIB Small Business Optimism

The National Federation of Independent Business Small Business Optimism Index is a composite of ten seasonally adjusted components indicating the health of small businesses in the U.S. The economists expect a reading of 94.5 in November, after it rose to 93.7 in October.

8:30 AM Productivity and Cost

Productivity and costs are economic indicators that measure the efficiency of a company's production process and the cost of producing goods and services

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement

Buybacks are a cash management tool to give the government flexibility to manage the public debt.

1:00 PM 3-Year Note Auction

The auction indicates the percentage yield on three 3-year US Treasury notes. The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $58 billion of 3-year notes.

According to over-the-counter interbank yield quotes for this government bond maturity, the US 3-year note bond yield was 4.08 percent on Monday, December 9.

2:00 PM Treasury Buyback Results

Institutions that are approved by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (FRBNY) to conduct open market transactions may participate in a buyback. FRBNY accepts offers with the best value relative to prevailing market prices. Shortly after the operation, the Treasury publishes summary results.

data.title: Gilead reports positive long-term data for Yescarta for NHL
data.description:
Gilead’s (NASDAQ:GILD) Kite unit reported positive results from a five-year follow-up of a Phase 2 study for its cell therapy Yescarta in certain patients with non-Hodgkin lymphoma, or NHL.

The analysis showed that after a median follow-up of more than five years, patients treated with Yescarta continued to experience durable response and long-term survival, according to a statement.

The study evaluated Yescarta in patients with relapsed/refractory non-NHL, including follicular lymphoma or marginal zone lymphoma.

The data was presented at the 66th American Society of Hematology Annual Meeting, which is being held Dec. 7-10 in San Diego.

Yescarta is approved to treat certain patients with B-cell lymphoma and follicular lymphoma.

data.title: Saudi oil price cut for Asia buyers underscores weakening market outlook
data.description:
Crude oil futures finished higher Monday after China eased its overall monetary policy stance for the first time in 14 years, and as the ouster of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad raises the political risk premium in the Middle East.

China's government pledged to embrace a "moderately loose" monetary policy next year, signaling more rate cuts ahead and shifting from a "prudent" strategy that had been maintained since 2010.

China's move to spark its lackluster economy could eventually support long-term demand, but analysts say the market's overall sentiment remains cautious for now, limiting substantial upside in oil prices.

Meanwhile, Assad's swift fall after years of civil war in Syria underlined the weakness of his key allies Russia and Iran, both major oil producers.

The events in Syria should "marginally" raise oil's geopolitical risk premium in the next few weeks, and the risk of regional spillover adds further uncertainty, Rystad Energy's Jorge Leon wrote.

The country is not a major oil producer, but it "holds huge geopolitical clout due to its location and ties with Russia and Iran," Leon said.

Ritterbusch said it expects the bounce in oil prices will be short-lived, since "connecting the dots between increased Middle East tensions and a disruption of oil supplies is difficult, and increasingly bearish oil balances will eventually win out in forcing lower prices."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery closed +1.7% to $68.37/bbl, and front-month February Brent crude (CO1:COM) finished +1.4% to $72.14/bbl, snapping a three-session losing streak for both benchmarks.

U.S. natural gas futures gained as weather forecasts turn colder for the latter part of this week, while the outlook remains relatively mild for rest of December; the Nymex January front-month contract (NG1:COM) ended +3.4% at $3.182/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Also weighing on prices, analysts say Saudi Arabia's decision to cut official oil selling prices for Asia underscores weaker demand in the continent, according to analysts at DNB Markets.

Aramco said it will sell January loadings of its flagship Arab Light grade to Asian buyers at a premium of $0.90/bbl to the regional Dubai/Oman benchmark, compared with $1.70/bbl in December.

"This is the lowest premium to Dubai since the start of 2021, and at the same time as OPEC has extended its production cuts," DNB analysts wrote. "Combined, this indicates demand softness in Asia."

data.title: Lilly reports positive Phase 3 data for Jaypirca in CLL/SLL
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) reported positive Phase 3 data from its BRUIN CLL-321 study of its drug pirtobrutunib, also known as Jaypirca, in the treatment adult patients with chronic lymphocytic leukemia, or CLL, or small lymphocytic lymphoma, or SLL, previously treated with a covalent BTK inhibitor.

The study met its primary endpoint of progression-free survival at primarily analysis, showing that pirtobrutinib was superior to idelalisib plus rituximab, also known as IdealR, or bendamustine plus rituximab, also known as BR, according to a statement.

Lilly (LLY) said the updated results demonstrate consistent improvement in progression-free survival for patients treated with pirtobrutinib, with a reduction in risk of relapse, disease or death by 46% compared to the IdelaR or BR combinations.

The data is expected to be presented at the 66th American Society of Hematology Annual Meeting, which is being held Dec. 7-10 in San Diego.

Jaypirca has received accelerated approval from the FDA for the treatment of adult patients with CLL/SLL who have received at least two prior lines of therapy, including a BTK inhibitor and BCL-2 inhibitor and adult patients with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma after at least two lines of systemic therapy, including a BTK inhibitor.

Lilly (LLY) noted that Jaypirca’s continued approval for the indications may be contingent upon verification and description of clinical benefit in a confirmatory trial. The drug was first approved in 2023.

data.title: Kinder Morgan rises after forecasting above-consensus 2025 earnings
data.description:
Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI) +1.6% post-market Monday after guiding for FY 2025 adjusted earnings of $1.27/share, above the $1.25 analyst consensus estimate and up 8% compared to its FY 2024 forecast of $1.17/share, while generating $8.3B of adjusted EBITDA, up nearly 4% from the 2024 forecast of $8B.

The company also projected an annualized dividend of $1.17/share in 2025, which would make the eighth straight year it has increased the dividend.

"Our end-of-year 2025 net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA ratio is forecast to be 3.8x, which is in the lower part of our 3.5x-4.5x leverage target range and provides good capacity for additional opportunistic investment," CEO Kim Dang said.

Kinder Morgan (KMI) added it expects to spend $2.3B in discretionary capital spending next year, including expansion projects and contributions to joint ventures.

The company said it expects to continue benefiting from strong natural gas market fundamentals driving growth on its existing natural gas transportation and storage assets, as well as creating expansion opportunities.

data.title: Gold futures jump on China central bank buying, more Middle East instability
data.description:
Gold futures rallied Monday as China's central bank resumed purchases of bullion in November after a six-month hiatus, which may shore up Chinese investor demand.

The People's Bank of China said it bought 160K fine troy ounces last month, its first addition since April, which was the end of an 18-month run of purchases that had helped underpin prices.

China was the world's largest official sector buyer of gold in 2023, and the bank's steady buying of bullion was one of the main factors that contributed to gold's record-breaking YTD rally.

"The market is getting hopeful that we could see other central banks follow suit and we could see a resumption of record territory buying," TD Securities head of commodity strategies Bart Melek said.

China also said it plans to be "more proactive" in stimulating its lackluster economy, which is bullish for metals from a demand perspective, since China is the leading global consumer of raw commodities.

Also helping gold is the anticipation of another interest rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve, with traders currently pricing an 87% chance of another 25 bps rate cut at the Fed’s December 17-18 meeting, up from 62% a week earlier.

The market will scrutinize Wednesday's U.S. Consumer Price Index report for further clues on the Fed's pathway to monetary policy easing.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery closed +1% to $2.664.90/oz, its highest settlement value since November 22, and front-month December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +3.3% to $32.216/oz, its best settlement value since November 5.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Gold also gained on safe-haven demand after Syrian rebels seized Damascus and ousted former President Bashar al-Assad, who has fled to Russia, and Israel's military moved to seize the demilitarized buffer zone between Israel and Syria over the weekend.

A softer U.S. dollar has further enhanced gold's appeal as a safe-haven investment.

data.title: The Container Store shares to be pulled from NYSE
data.description:
Trading in shares of The Container Store (NYSE:TCS) have been suspended immediately after the New York Stock Exchange said it will begin proceedings to delist the stock from the NYSE.

The decision was reached as the company’s global market capitalization had been below the $15M threshold for 30 consecutive trading days.

The decision is just the latest blow to the beleaguered company as it teeters on the brink of bankruptcy. A potential lifeline from Beyond (BYON) unraveled last month after TCS was unable to satisfy the terms of Beyond’s (BYON) equity investment. Unsuccessful efforts to obtain additional working capital or refinance the terms of its debt raised concerns about the company’s viability and eventually drove shares to an all-time low of $1.79, a loss of 92% from last year.

The company has struggled to regain profitability since the pandemic, as the challenges within the housing market coupled with consumers opting for cheaper options from Amazon (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT) pushed the company to the verge of bankruptcy.

“There will always be a cheaper alternative to a plastic bin,” Container Store CEO Satish Malhotra conceded.

To discourage a hostile takeover, The Container Store adopted a poison pill, and then announced a potential partnership with Beyond (BYON) that would help restore TCS profitability by leveraging Beyond’s intellectual property, customer data, brand network, and affiliate relationship.

However, the deal was contingent on The Container Store refinancing or amending its borrowing terms with creditors. Unable to obtain commercially acceptable financing to satisfy the terms of the purchase agreement, Beyond (BYON) pulled out of the deal, leaving The Container Store scrambling – unsuccessfully -- to find financing to fund operations.

In the fiscal second quarter, The Container Store (TCS) reported a loss of $3.23 per share (versus a profit of $0.11 a year ago) and a 10.5% drop in sales as comparable stores sales fell 12.5% from a year earlier.

data.title: Momentus announces 1-for-14 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Momentus (NASDAQ:MNTS) has effectuated a 1-for-14 reverse stock split of its Class A common stock that will become effective on December 12, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time.
  • The company’s common stock will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis at the opening of the market on December 13, 2024.
  • The reverse stock split will reduce the number of outstanding shares from ~31M shares to ~2M shares.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to bring the company into compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement.
  • MNTS shares were up 1% in after hours trading on Monday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: California's outlook boosted to stable as fiscal issues ease: Moody's
data.description:
Moody's Ratings upgraded the State of California's outlook to stable from negative on Monday on the view that "the state's fiscal challenges have eased in light of both the implementation of spending adjustments and favorable revenue performance supported by a strong economy."

State reserves have retreated from record highs, but are expected to remain adequate as California progresses through fiscal 2025 and into fiscal 2026, Moody's said in a statement.

"We expect the state will navigate future budget gaps, which are likely, by making sufficient adjustments to revenue and spending so as to maintain a satisfactory financial profile."

At the same time, Moody's reaffirmed California's Aa2 issuer rating.
